figures.caption: Table 6: Backbone generalization. Δ denotes the absolute gain of using ckpt-3000 over Original under the same inference method, measuring RL training’s contribution.

Figure 1: Overview of the AdaFocus framework. (a) Coarse Preview Stage: the input video is processed at 1 fps through a CLIP encoder and the AdaRD sampler to produce a compact keyframe set K ≪ 𝑁 , which is fed to the backbone LVLM as visual tokens. (b) Confidence Gating: the frozen LVLM generates an answer; if the length-normalized log-probability confidence 𝐶 ≥ 𝜏 the answer is accepted immediately, otherwise an agentic look-back is triggered. (c) Zero-Cache Refinement: the target timestamp is grounded via regex parsing or cross-attention fallback, and a high-resolution temporal window [𝑡 − Δ𝑤, 𝑡 + Δ𝑤] is decoded directly from disk via zero-cache I/O and fused into the evidence set for re-generation, achieving ∼33× token savings over dense oracle encoding.

Figure 2: Detailed methodology of AdaFocus. (a) Relevance-Diversity Balancing: each candidate frame is scored by cosine similarity 𝑟𝑖 = 𝑆𝑟𝑒𝑙 (𝑣𝑖 ) with a temporal diversity penalty; adaptive routing switches to global clustering when 𝑔(V,Q) < 𝜏𝑔𝑙𝑜𝑏𝑎𝑙 , producing a compressed preview K . (b) Uncertainty Mechanism: the backbone LVLM generates tokens whose log-probabilities are monitored against a length-calibrated threshold 𝛾 (𝐿) = 𝛾0 − 𝛽 log(𝐿); falling below the threshold triggers refinement (𝑧 = 1). (c) Zero-Cache Grounding & Fallback: the target timestamp is first extracted via regex parsing (𝑡𝑟𝑒𝑔𝑒𝑥 ); if unavailable, a crossattention fallback selects the most-attended frame (𝑡𝑎𝑡𝑡𝑛 = argmax𝑖 𝐴𝑖 ). The retrieved window is fused into the evidence set E𝑓 𝑖𝑛𝑎𝑙 = EK ∪ ER without any VRAM pre-cache.
